data.title: Former energy secretary Rick Perry's data center REIT launches IPO roadshow
data.description:
Data center REIT Fermi (FRMI) announced on Wednesday the launch of a roadshow for its proposed IPO of 25M shares, expected to be priced between $18 and $22 per share.

The data center REIT was co-founded by the former U.S. Energy Secretary Rick Perry and the co-founder of Quantum Energy (OTCPK:QREE), Toby Neugebauer.

The company develops electric grids that deliver highly redundant power at gigawatt scale, required to create next-generation artificial intelligence.

A registration statement relating to the securities has been filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission, but has not yet become effective.

Also called Fermi America, the company intends to grant the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 3.75M shares at the IPO price.

The company intends to list its shares on the Nasdaq Global Select Market and the London Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol FRMI.

Net proceeds are intended to be used to support the development of business, secure personnel, and increase financial flexibility, and for general corporate purposes.

data.title: Packaging Corp. of America rated Overweight in new coverage at J.P. Morgan
data.description:
Packaging Corp. of America (NYSE:PKG) is well positioned to capitalize on structural changes in the corrugated container industry, according to financial-services firm J.P. Morgan. Analyst Detlef Winckelmann on Wednesday initiated coverage with an Overweight rating, highlighting PKG’s market leadership, favorable supply-side dynamics and strong balance sheet.

Shares of PKG rose 1.4% by 10:41 a.m. ET Wednesday.

Although PKG is the nation’s third-largest box producer, Winckelmann noted it is considered the leader “on virtually every metric.” The U.S. corrugated sector, he argued, is undergoing a shift as competitors focus on value over volume, a strategy that could ultimately shrink capacity and allow PKG to capture market share.

“Peers International Paper (NYSE:IP) and Smurfit WestRock (NYSE:SW) are both under new management and have chosen to embark on value over volume strategies for their box businesses,” Winckelmann wrote. “The most likely outcomes of these new strategies, in our view, are peers losing box volumes to PKG and peer paper integration declining. Given this is undesirable, the most likely outcome seems to be mill capacity closures.”

With operating rates already at 94% to 95%, J.P. Morgan expects limited new supply and even potential net closures in the short to medium term. This could tighten the market despite demand levels that remain below historical averages.

PKG also benefits from financial flexibility. Following its $1.8 billion acquisition of Greif, the company’s net debt is about two times earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization. Winckelmann projects that figure will decline to around 1.3 times by 2026, creating room for shareholder returns. He estimates PKG could support annual buybacks of $500 million, roughly 2.5% of its market capitalization, by 2027 or sooner.

While PKG is the firm’s preferred U.S. pure play, J.P. Morgan also maintained a bullish stance on Smurfit WestRock (NYSE:SW). Winckelmann sees approximately 65% of its ebitda exposed to U.S. market tailwinds, with management guiding $800 million to $1.2 billion in incremental box business ebitda over the next five years. Consensus forecasts, he noted, imply far lower gains, creating what he called “a low bar to beat expectations.” At about 6.5 times enterprise value-to-ebitda, Smurfit (SW) trades below International Paper (NYSE:IP), which is valued above eight times.

“PKG is uniquely placed to benefit from these dynamics and the valuation looks attractive, especially as our blue-sky scenario appears to be becoming increasingly likely,” Winckelmann concluded.

J.P. Morgan set a December 2026 price target of $242 a share, using an equal weighting of a one-year forward enterprise value-to-ebitda multiple valuation and a one-year forward free cash flow yield multiple valuation.

data.title: Copper futures jump as Freeport hit with Grasberg mine suspension impact
data.description:
Copper prices jumped more than 3% Wednesday after Freeport-McMoRan (FCX) detailed the significant production impact from the suspension of its Grasberg Block Cave mine in Indonesia following this month's deadly mud rush incident.

Production in the Grasberg minerals district - the world's second-largest copper mining complex - was stopped following the incident, and Freeport (FCX) said preliminary assessments indicate the impacts likely will result in the deferral of significant production in Q4 2025 and full-year 2026, with "a return to pre-incident operating rates potentially achieved in 2027."

Nymex copper futures (HG1:COM) shot up immediately on the update, reversing an earlier decline, and recently traded +3.2% to $4.739/lb.

Freeport's (FCX) disclosure is the latest disruption to the copper industry, after Hudbay Minerals (HBM) said it is shutting operations at a mill at its Constancia mine site in Peru due to ongoing political protests.

While Freeport-McMoran (FCX) sinks to its lowest in nearly two months, -10.6%, Southern Copper (SCCO), Hudbay (HBM) and Teck Resources (TECK) rallied +9.5%, +6.1% and +5.1%, respectively.

In London, Glencore (OTCPK:GLCNF) (OTCPK:GLNCY) shares surged as much as 3.5% while Antofagasta (OTC:ANFGF) added 8.7%.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:CPER), (NYSEARCA:COPX), (OTC:JJCTF)

data.title: LTC Properties raised to Market Perform at BMO on senior housing growth
data.description:
BMO Capital Markets upgraded LTC Properties (NYSE:LTC) to Market Perform from Underperform as growth at its senior housing operating portfolio ("SHOP") is expected to pick up.

"At its current cost of capital, we believe SHOP acquisition accretion is limited, but earnings growth should be enhanced, which may drive multiple expansion," analyst Juan Sanabria wrote in a note to clients.

LTC Properties (NYSE:LTC) stock rose 1.6% in Wednesday morning trading.

SHOP is expected to comprise ~20% of the pro forma portfolio, with acquisition execution being key. "LTC's balance sheet is well-positioned with potential valuation upside vs. peers," he added.

While M&A is possible, it's unlikely near term, Sanabria said.

BMO's Market Perform rating contrasts with the SA Quant rating of Strong Buy and agrees with the average SA Analyst rating and average Wall Street rating, both at Hold.

data.title: Wall Street struggles for traction amid mixed tech stocks, rate-cut uncertainties
data.description:
Wall Street's major indexes seesawed and were little changed on Wednesday, as U.S. technology stocks weighed on the markets and uncertainty persisted among investors.

The S&P 500 (SP500) was last flat in late morning trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was also unmoved, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.1%.

“Investors trimmed their exposure following a comment from Fed Chair Jerome Powell [saying] ‘equity prices are fairly highly valued.’ He went on to say that the current situation wasn’t a time ‘… of elevated financial stability risks,’ but investors decided that discretion is the better part of valour and took the opportunity to lighten up a touch,” said David Morrison, senior market analyst at Trade Nation.

On the tech front, OpenAI is considering renting AI chips from Nvidia (NVDA) -0.2% to reduce costs associated with the new $100B data center partnership between the two companies.

“This investment was welcomed in many quarters as evidence of the faith that the most valuable company in history has in the future of generative AI. But others have questioned the circular nature of the investment, particularly as the journey to monetizing the ongoing funding in AI is far from clear,” said Morrison.

“On top of this, the assumption is that Nvidia (NVDA), and other leading chip companies, have an unbridgeable moat around them. But as was demonstrated with DeepSeek at the beginning of this year, it’s quite possible that Chinese companies come up with cheaper alternatives to Nvidia’s products, particularly since U.S. bans on selling specific chips to China are driving Chinese innovation in this area,” he added.

Micron Technology (MU) edged lower -2%, even after its outlook eclipsed estimates on AI data center demand. Adobe (ADBE) -3% and Oracle (ORCL) -2.5% are also pushing the S&P 500 (SP500) and the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) lower.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.13%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 3.60%.

On the economy, new home sales surged 20.5% MoM in August to 800k. Consensus was for 649k. Also, the Survey of Business Uncertainty will arrive shortly.

U.S. stocks ended in the red on Tuesday, breaking from their three-day all-time high closes, after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell largely reiterated his message from last week that policymakers will have a difficult path ahead on interest rates.

data.title: Baidu’s Apollo Go begins Dubai's first autonomous ride-hailing trials
data.description:
Baidu’s (NASDAQ:BIDU) (OTC:BAIDF) Apollo Go received Dubai’s first autonomous driving trial permit and 50 test licenses from the RTA.

Apollo Go is the only platform currently authorized for self-driving trials on Dubai’s public roads. Autonomous vehicle trials began in August with a 50-vehicle fleet operating on open roads in designated Dubai areas. 

Baidu plans to expand the fleet to over 1,000 fully driverless vehicles in Dubai by 2028. This move follows a March partnership and memorandum of understanding with Dubai's RTA.

Apollo Go previously became the first to receive an autonomous test license in Hong Kong in Nov 2024.

Baidu’s autonomous platform has logged 200 million safe kilometers and provided 14 million autonomous rides in China.

Source: Press release

data.title: Astera Labs' stock tumbles nearly 11%
data.description:
Shares of Astera Labs (NASDAQ:ALAB) tumbled about 11% on Wednesday, and the company is potentially poised to shed about $4B in market cap.

Astera Labs — which develops semiconductor-based connectivity solutions for cloud and AI infrastructure — fell up to nearly 13% on Wednesday, its biggest intraday fall since April 4.

The stock has declined about 3% each day since Sept. 19, after Morgan Stanley said that Intel's (INTC) deal with Nvidia (NVDA) to co-develop PC and data center chips could have major implications for Astera, but it is unclear exactly what they are at this point.

As of the close on Sept. 23, Astera's stock has soared about 55% year-to-date, while in the past six months the shares have surged around 178%.

In the past one year, the stock has jumped about 300%.

data.title: Alcohol lobby takes on WHO in battle over health impacts - report
data.description:
The Belgian beer industry, Mexico's tequila makers, and Heineken (OTCQX:HEINY) reportedly all lobbied governments this summer to resist a push by the U.N.'s health agency to introduce tougher rules targeting alcohol, letters, and an email reviewed by Reuters show.

Julian Braithwaite, CEO of the International Alliance for Responsible Drinking (IARD), told Reuters the industry aims to "take back control of the alcohol debate."

To that end, IARD's members, which include leading brewers and spirit makers, have increased the organization's funding to have it play a more active role in alcohol-related science debates and counter the message coming from health bodies, the report said.

As financially stretched and more health-conscious consumers reduce alcohol consumption, hurting company profits, the standoff highlights how the sector is intensifying efforts to counter further challenges.

In March, Diageo (DEO), (OTCPK:DGEAF) advertised for a new role on its global lobbying team, citing "an unprecedented challenge" from the WHO and related non-governmental organisations pressuring governments.

Such roles are a "standard part of how we engage with governments to advocate for proportionate, evidence-based policies that support both society and business", according to a Diageo spokesperson.

Diageo interim CEO Nik Jhangiani said at a recent conference that the spirits sector was being "outshone" by negative messaging, and it should do more to manage the dialogue.

And Carlsberg (OTCPK:CABGY) CEO Jacob Aarup-Andersen told Reuters last month that moderate drinking has benefits for socializing and mental health and said the industry should communicate those.

data.title: Bessent says Fed should have signaled 100-150 basis points in cuts - report
data.description:
U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said Wednesday in a broadcast interview that Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell should have signaled that the central bank will cut its policy rate by 100-150 basis points more, on the basis that the federal funds rate has been too high for too long.

"Rates are too restrictive. They need to come down," he said on Fox Business's "Mornings with Maria" program. He pointed to downward revisions to jobs numbers as justification for lowering interest rates. "With these revisions, we know that something was wrong beneath the hood."

"I'm a bit surprised that the chair hasn't signaled that we have a destination before the end of the year of at least 100 to 150 basis points," he said. 

Bessent's remarks come after Powell indicated on Tuesday that all options are open, and emphasized that there's no risk-free path for policy as risks to the economic outlook, both on the employment side and the price stability side of its mandates, have increased. If the Fed eases too much or too quickly, inflation can resurge. If it keeps rates restrictive for too long, it can do unnecessary damage to the labor market.

Meanwhile, Stephen Miran, the newest Fed governor and a Trump ally, on Monday said his analysis suggests that the federal funds rate range is about 2 percentage points too high.

Miran said his projection for the year-end fed funds rate was the lowest on the dot plot at last week's meeting, which was halfway between 2.75%-3.00%. The next lowest dots were at between 3.5%-3.75%.

Bessent also commented on Miran's appointment to the Fed, saying "it's good to get some new blood in there." Not unlike the UN, he suggested that there's been "a lot of mistakes, a lot of rigidity" at the central bank.

Regarding new blood at the Fed, the Treasury Secretary said he's continuing to interview the 11 candidates for the new Fed chair. "We've got a couple who have surprised me in the strength of their candidacy," Bessent said, though he declined to name them. The first round of interviews should be completed in the first week of October, then he'll start another round, before presenting to President Donald Trump three or four "very strong candidates," he said.

While Bessent and Miran are urging large cuts to the Fed's policy rate, other Federal Open Market Committee members are being more cautious. Fed Governor Michelle Bowman, who has been arguing for a rate cut for June, said that her focus has shifted to risks to employment, a move that typically favors further rate cuts, but she hasn't suggested large cuts at this point. St. Louis Fed President Alberto Musalem, though, sees limited room for more cuts as inflation remains higher than the Fed's 2% goal, and Atlanta Fed President Raphael Bostic is hesitant to cut further.

With the softening in the labor market, Bessent said he's less concerned about a recession than he is about the distributional aspect. "I'm concerned that working Americans are getting hit harder just because of the higher rates," he said. "I believe that we do need rate relief to get at least back to neutral."

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Wednesday: Chinese tech stocks, LAC, QURE, and more
data.description:
Stock futures inched higher on Wednesday, recovering from a slight pullback in the prior session. Investor sentiment remains cautious, however, after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell warned about the difficult balance the Fed faces in controlling persistent inflation while avoiding further labor market weakness.

Here are some of Wednesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • uniQure (QURE) +185% - Shares skyrocketed after its Huntington’s disease gene therapy AMT-130 met primary and key secondary endpoints in a pivotal Phase 2/3 trial, showing a significant slowdown in disease progression at 36 months. The company plans to file for FDA approval in Q1 2026. ClearPoint Neuro (CLPT) +45% also rallied, as its delivery tech was used in the therapy.
  • Lithium Americas (NYSE:LAC) +91 - Shares surged after reports surfaced that the Trump administration is seeking up to a 10% equity stake in the company while renegotiating terms of its $2.3B Department of Energy loan for the $3B Thacker Pass lithium project. The project, approved by Trump in his first term, closed its DoE loan under the Biden administration, but officials are now pushing for revised terms amid weak lithium prices and repayment concerns.
  • Alibaba Group (BABA) +9% - Chinese tech stocks rallied, led by Alibaba after the company boosted AI investment plans beyond $50B and launched its largest-ever language model, Qwen3-Max. ADRs of peers also advanced: Baidu (BIDU) +6%, JD.com (JD) +7%, Bilibili (BILI) +3%, Baozun (BZUN) +1%, and Trip.com (TCOM) +1.3%. The Hang Seng Tech Index gained 2.7%, now up 46% YTD on AI-driven momentum.
  • ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW) +3% - Shares advanced after Morgan Stanley upgraded the stock to Overweight from Equal-Weight, citing its ability to sustain ~20% subscription growth and attractive long-term FCF potential. Analyst Keith Weiss pointed to the company’s strong leadership, horizontal workflow automation strategy, and GenAI tailwinds, setting a $1,250 PT (~35% upside) despite shares being down 12% YTD.

Biggest stock losers

  • Worthington Enterprises (WOR) -14% - Shares slumped despite FQ1 beats on sales ($303.7M vs. $291.5M est.) and EPS ($0.74 vs. $0.70 est.), as investors focused on weaker cash flow (-12% Y/Y to $27.9M) and higher costs tied to acquisitions and facility upgrades. Building product sales jumped 32% on higher volumes and the Elgen acquisition, but consumer products saw weaker demand, pressuring margins. Operating income improved to $9.2M, yet margins remain thin at 3%.
  • Cohu (NASDAQ:COHU) -9% - Shares sank after the company announced a $200M private offering of convertible senior notes due 2031, with an option for purchasers to buy an additional $30M. Proceeds will be used to fund capped call transactions and for general corporate purposes.
  • Micron Technology (MU) -2% - Shares slipped despite initially gaining in premarket trading after posting strong FQ4 results and upbeat FQ1 guidance. EPS is projected at $3.60–$3.90 vs. $3.10 consensus, and revenue at $12.2B–$12.8B vs. $11.91B consensus. CEO Sanjay Mehrotra pointed to record FY25 data center performance and said Micron enters FY26 with its “most competitive portfolio to date,” well-positioned to capture AI-driven demand.
